growth_records.cite: Gassler et al. Induced endosymbiosis between a fungus and bacterium reveals a shift from antagonism to commensalism. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-65741-9. [PMC12663227]
growth_records.method: calculated (growth_rate_per_h = ln2/doubling_time_h = ln2/1.7h ≈ 0.41 h-1); doubling_time_h itself is reported directly in the paper
growth_records.snippet: The intracellular maximum doubling time (TDmax) of R. pickettii after injection is approximately 1.7 h; for determination a single bacterial cell was followed by timelapse microscopy directly after injection and imaged f
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h calculated as ln2/1.7h = 0.41 h-1 from the paper's directly reported intracellular doubling time (TDmax approximately 1.7 h), determined by exponential fit to single bacterial cell volume (voxel, arbitrary units) over a 15 h time-lapse imaging series immediately after FluidFM injection into the cytosol of an endosymbiont-free (NH) Rhizopus microsporus germling. This is NOT a conventional defined-medium batch/chemostat measurement -- it is single-cell intracellular (endosymbiotic) growth after experimental host-cell injection, hence culture_mode/oxygen/temperature/pH for the growth measurement itself are not stated by the authors (left null/described qualitatively) and confidence is medium. The paper additionally states this intracellular rate is 'similar to in vitro growth rates in liquid medium and on agar' but does not give a numeric in vitro value, so no separate free-living growth-rate record could be made. Routine stock culture (separate from the measured condition) of R. pickettii K-288 was in Nutrient-Broth medium at 28°C in shake flasks (implying aerobic), per Methods, but no rate was reported for that condition. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature]
